sub_mechanisms: M_1: define K token-type categories (e.g., entity, verb, function, numeric, rare)
M_2: construct a Latin-square allocation: token-type x batch-position; each cell holds one token-instance
M_3: form B mini-batches that across the schedule cycle have balanced K-by-position exposure (BIBD style)
M_4: train using the scheduled batches; expect lower variance in per-token-type loss
stripped_llm_application: A mini-batch construction strategy for LLM pretraining that uses a Latin-square / BIBD schedule to balance token-type by batch-position exposure across a training epoch. The schedule guarantees each (token-type, position) pair appears equally often within a cycle.
novelty_claim_stripped: Standard mini-batch construction is random with weighted sampling. Curriculum-learning literature schedules by difficulty. The candidate adds an algebraic-design layer (Latin-square / BIBD) to balance token-type-by-position structure orthogonally to difficulty.
